metadata.title:
DEADLY WRECK
ON THE GREAT


NORTHERN

pages.raw_text:
The Great Northern is having
run of bad luck lately in the
operation of it's line on both sides
of the mountains. Near Monroe, a
Snohomish county town on the
west side, freight and local pas
senger trains collided yesterday,
the accident resulting in the death
of Engineer ]. E. Hudson. Fireman
A. w. Riddell and Patrick Sheri-
dan, another trainman.


George Lawrence and Wetzell,
engineer and conductor of the pas
senger train, were injured.


Failure on the part of the
freight crew to obey orders is
given as the cause of the collision.
The crew had orders to wait for
the local at Monroe, but pulled out
in the belief that they could reach
Sultan in time to pass the local
there. A breakdown delayed the
freig t after leaving Monroe and
the collision came shortly after
the freight pulled up again, the
trains meeting with terrific force
on a curve. Passengers were badly
shaken up, but none seriously in
jured.


At the instance of the coroner,
Conductor Erickson, of the freight,
was arrested at Everett to be held
for the result of the inquest this
afternoon.


For the two wrecks that occurred
at Malaga and Trinidad, on this
side of the mountains, Saturday,
company officials are still unable to
assign any reason. At Trinidad
freight train ran into siding on
